steps.title: Combine both reasons
steps.content: The text explains scholarly interest in juvenilia through two examples. First, unpublished works by writers who became famous, such as Auden's poem, can show artistic development. Then the text adds a second reason: juvenilia by lesser-known writers, such as Ashford's novels, can matter because those works were read by contemporaries and help scholars understand literary history.

steps.title: Reject narrower purposes
steps.content: Choice D covers the whole passage because it says the text presents reasons scholars value juvenilia as resources. Choice A invents challenges famous writers faced when trying to publish childhood works. Choice B claims Ashford's novels have more merit than Auden's juvenilia, but the passage does not rank them. Choice C shifts to a comparison between young writers and adult contemporaries, not the scholarly value of juvenilia. The answer is D.
